content.problemStatement:
From a hidden array AAA of length NNN, Stack constructs an array BBB of length NNN such that:


For all iii (1≤i≤N)(1 \leq i \leq N)(1≤i≤N), Bi=max⁡(A1,A2,…,Ai)B_i=\max(A_1,A_2,\ldots,A_i)Bi​=max(A1​,A2​,…,Ai​) **or** Bi=min⁡(A1,A2,…,Ai)B_i=\min(A_1,A_2,\ldots,A_i)Bi​=min(A1​,A2​,…,Ai​).
For the given array BBB of length NNN, Stack wants you to check whether a possible array AAA exists or not.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the array BBB.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1,B_2,\ldots,B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​ representing the array BBB.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line `YES` if AAA exists, otherwise print `NO`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, `yes`, and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the length of array B. The second line contains N space‑separated integers B1 … BN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if there exists at least one array A that can generate the given B, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: A=[343] works. Test case 2: A can be [1,1,2,3] giving prefix max each step. Test case 3: No possible A because B3=2 lies between current max=3 and min=1.
First case: all elements equal to 1. Second case: one feasible A is [1,2,1,1,1]; the sequence of B alternates between max and min as allowed.
Start with curMax=curMin=5. B2=5 OK. B3=4 ≤ curMin, curMin becomes 4. B4=4 OK. B5=6 ≥ curMax, curMax becomes 6. B6=2 is neither ≥6 nor ≤4, so impossible.

solution.approach: We construct the array A from left to right while keeping the current prefix maximum (curMax) and prefix minimum (curMin). For i = 1 we must have A1 = B1, therefore curMax = curMin = B1. For each subsequent i we need Bi to be either the new prefix maximum or the new prefix minimum. This is possible iff Bi ≥ curMax (we can make Bi the new maximum by setting Ai = Bi) or Bi ≤ curMin (we can make Bi the new minimum by setting Ai = Bi). If Bi lies strictly between curMin and curMax, no choice of Ai can satisfy the condition, and the answer is "NO". When Bi satisfies one of the two inequalities we update curMax or curMin accordingly (if Bi expands the range). If we finish the whole array without contradiction, the answer is "YES". The algorithm is a single linear scan per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(ΣN) overall (≤ 10^5).
